mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high PAYEMS environments fall below the 20-day moving average for 3 consecutive weeks.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) signal increased demand for construction and related sectors, generating positive S&P 500 momentum over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high HOUST environments show negative correlation with housing data (Spearman rho < -0.2 over 12-month window).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 315 (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns exceed 1.5% in 40%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 315.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signal supply-side bottlenecks, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between INDPRO and HOUST shows positive correlation with SPX returns (beta > 0.15 in 60-day rolling regression).

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) create headwinds for equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns exceed 5% in 30%+ of FEDFUNDS > 4.5% periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) reflects weak manufacturing activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show positive correlation with INDPRO (Spearman rho > 0.25 over 12-month window).

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS) boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in high RSAFS environments fall below the 200-day moving average for 6 consecutive months.
